engines_involved.role: conditional — only fires when alert severity is critical or high

trigger_sources: AlertTriageEngine after ingesting an alert (if it emits ALERT_CREATED)
on_threat_detected() fan-out (if ALERT_CREATED is emitted after alert creation)
on_anomaly_detected() fan-out (if ALERT_CREATED is emitted after alert creation)

registration.legacy_event_type: NOT REGISTERED — ALERT_CREATED is absent from legacy bus type_map

current_state.summary: Single-engine conditional flow. IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() is called only for critical/high alerts. The incident type is hardcoded 'other'. source_engine is passed through as 'source' on the incident data. Title is prefixed 'Escalated: ' to distinguish auto-escalated incidents from manually created ones.
current_state.circular_risk: CRITICAL DESIGN ISSUE: on_threat_detected() (THREAT_DETECTED handler) itself creates an alert via AlertTriageEngine. If AlertTriageEngine emits ALERT_CREATED after ingestion, and that ALERT_CREATED triggers on_alert_created(), which creates an incident, AND the incident creation emits INCIDENT_CREATED, the chain is: THREAT_DETECTED → alert → ALERT_CREATED → incident → INCIDENT_CREATED → cost tracking. This is the intended cascade but is not documented or tested. If AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() does NOT emit ALERT_CREATED, then on_alert_created() is never triggered by the THREAT_DETECTED path.
current_state.alert_created_emission_gap: AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() does not appear to emit ALERT_CREATED on the event bus, meaning on_alert_created() currently only fires if an external engine explicitly emits ALERT_CREATED.

what_works: Severity gate correctly limits incident creation to critical/high alerts.
source_engine is forwarded to the incident record.
Severity is re-validated before passing to IncidentOrchestrationEngine with fallback 'high'.
what_doesnt: ALERT_CREATED is missing from the legacy EventBus type_map — legacy-bus emitters will not trigger this subscriber.

AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() does not currently emit ALERT_CREATED on the event bus after storing the alert, so the auto-escalation chain is broken for alerts created by THREAT_DETECTED and ANOMALY_DETECTED subscribers.
No deduplication — the same alert can trigger multiple incident creations if ALERT_CREATED fires more than once.
Incident type is always 'other' — no mapping from alert source or title to a meaningful incident type (e.g. 'malware', 'phishing', 'data_breach').
The created incident_id is never stored or returned to the caller — no linkage back to the originating alert.
No INCIDENT_CREATED emission is verified — IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() must emit INCIDENT_CREATED for the cost-tracking chain to fire.
For medium/low/info alerts, there is no downstream action at all — no ticket, no enrichment, nothing.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given ALERT_CREATED with severity='critical', IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() is called exactly once with the correct org_id and title prefixed 'Escalated: '.
AC2: Given ALERT_CREATED with severity='medium', IncidentOrchestrationEngine must NOT be called.
AC3: AlertTriageEngine.ingest_alert() must emit an ALERT_CREATED event on the TrustGraph bus after successfully storing the alert, carrying org_id, alert_id, severity, and source_engine.
AC4: ALERT_CREATED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map in register_cross_category_subscribers().
AC5: The incident created by this handler must store alert_id as a structured field linking back to the originating alert.
AC6: IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() must emit an INCIDENT_CREATED event carrying org_id and incident_id so the cost-tracking chain fires.
AC7: A pytest test verifies: critical path triggers incident, medium path does not, and the alert_id → incident linkage is present.
AC8: Failure of IncidentOrchestrationEngine must be caught and logged; the caller must not receive an exception.
